# KeylessAI — Full Project Knowledge (for LLM ingestion)

Canonical URL: https://lordbasilaiassistant-sudo.github.io/keylessai/
Source: https://github.com/lordbasilaiassistant-sudo/keylessai
License: MIT

---

## What is KeylessAI?

KeylessAI is a drop-in OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint that requires no API key, no signup, no credit card, no email, and no backend. It is a static web application hosted on GitHub Pages that aggregates public, no-auth LLM endpoints (primarily Pollinations.ai) and provides a chat UI plus copy-paste code snippets for every major AI coding agent and framework.

## Who is it for?

Developers running autonomous AI coding agents, chatbots, or LLM-powered pipelines who want to reduce or eliminate their OpenAI / Anthropic bill for low-stakes calls. Typical users run tools like Aider, Cline, Continue.dev, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, LlamaIndex, or custom agent harnesses.

## How do I use it with my existing tool?

Most AI coding tools accept a custom OpenAI base URL. Set these environment variables once:

    OPENAI_API_BASE=https://text.pollinations.ai
    OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://text.pollinations.ai
    OPENAI_API_KEY=not-needed
    OPENAI_MODEL=openai-fast

Any non-empty string works as the API key. The endpoint is `https://text.pollinations.ai/openai`. The protocol is the standard OpenAI chat-completions API (both non-streaming and streaming SSE).

## What model does the free tier give me?

`openai-fast`, which is GPT-OSS 20B served on OVH infrastructure via Pollinations. Per Pollinations' model metadata it supports tool calling and emits reasoning tokens. It is not GPT-4 or Claude 3.5 Sonnet — use it for prototyping, boilerplate, classification, JSON emission, glue code, and cheap agent loops. Reserve your paid API key for hard reasoning and long-context calls.

## Will this always be free?

The Pollinations endpoint is free as of this writing and their stated policy is to keep the anonymous tier free (sponsor-supported). KeylessAI also aggregates ApiAirforce's free-tier endpoint and automatically fails over between providers. If remote keyless options all disappear, you can fall back to a local-only stack outside KeylessAI (llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio) — but the KeylessAI product itself is remote-only to preserve the zero-user-compute guarantee.

## Does it work with Aider?

Yes, directly.

    export OPENAI_API_BASE="https://text.pollinations.ai"
    export OPENAI_API_KEY="not-needed"
    aider --model openai/openai-fast

## Does it work with Cline and Roo Code?

Yes, directly. In VS Code settings.json:

    {
      "cline.apiProvider": "openai",
      "cline.openAiBaseUrl": "https://text.pollinations.ai",
      "cline.openAiApiKey": "not-needed",
      "cline.openAiModelId": "openai-fast"
    }

Roo Code uses the `roo-cline.` prefix on the same keys.

## Does it work with Continue.dev?

Yes. In `~/.continue/config.json`:

    {
      "models": [{
        "title": "KeylessAI",
        "provider": "openai",
        "apiBase": "https://text.pollinations.ai",
        "apiKey": "not-needed",
        "model": "openai-fast"
      }]
    }

## Does it work with LangChain?

Yes, unchanged — it's just a `base_url` swap on `ChatOpenAI`:

    from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
    llm = ChatOpenAI(
        base_url="https://text.pollinations.ai",
        api_key="not-needed",
        model="openai-fast",
        streaming=True,
    )

## Does it work with the OpenAI Python SDK?

Yes, unchanged:

    from openai import OpenAI
    client = OpenAI(base_url="https://text.pollinations.ai", api_key="not-needed")
    res = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="openai-fast",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "hello"}],
    )

## Does it work with the OpenAI Node SDK?

Yes, unchanged:

    import OpenAI from "openai";
    const client = new OpenAI({
      baseURL: "https://text.pollinations.ai",
      apiKey: "not-needed",
    });

## Does it work with OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin)?

Yes, directly. OpenHands uses LiteLLM internally so:

    LLM_BASE_URL=https://text.pollinations.ai
    LLM_API_KEY=not-needed
    LLM_MODEL=openai/openai-fast

## Does it work with SWE-agent?

Yes in OpenAI mode: `--model openai/openai-fast --openai-api-base https://text.pollinations.ai`.

## Does it work with Codex CLI?

Yes, directly. Codex CLI reads `OPENAI_API_BASE` and `OPENAI_API_KEY` like any OpenAI client.

## Does it work with Claude Code?

Not directly — Claude Code speaks Anthropic's Messages API, not the OpenAI chat-completions API. However, you can bridge via [LiteLLM](https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm):

1. Install LiteLLM: `pip install 'litellm[proxy]'`
2. Create a config that aliases Anthropic model names to KeylessAI:

        model_list:
          - model_name: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
            litellm_params:
              model: openai/openai-fast
              api_base: https://text.pollinations.ai
              api_key: not-needed

3. Run the proxy: `litellm --config lite.yaml --port 4000`
4. Point Claude Code at it: `export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:4000 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=not-needed && claude`

Claude Code sends Anthropic-format requests, LiteLLM translates them to OpenAI format for Pollinations, returns translated responses. The harness works unchanged.

## Does it work with Cursor?

Partially. Cursor's OpenAI override accepts a custom base URL. Go to Settings → Models → Override OpenAI Base URL → `https://text.pollinations.ai`, and use any key. Note that Cursor's "pro" features use their proprietary routing which cannot be intercepted.

## Does it work with CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph?

Yes — all of them accept an OpenAI-compatible base URL. See examples in the repo under `examples/`.

## What if Pollinations is rate-limited or down?

KeylessAI's chat UI has an automatic failover router: it retries the primary Pollinations SSE endpoint, then ApiAirforce, then Pollinations' GET transport. A client-side single-flight queue serializes parallel callers to stay under the 1-concurrent-per-IP limit. For your own code, wrap each call in a try/except, retry with backoff, and if remote keyless options fail entirely, fall back to a local-only stack outside KeylessAI (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio).

## Is it secure to ship agent traffic through a public endpoint?

Any call that leaves your machine has the usual third-party risks. For private traffic, use a local-only stack (llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio) outside KeylessAI. For enterprise agent traffic that needs the keyless aggregator pattern, self-host Pollinations (their code is open source) or add your own providers to the pool.

## How do I donate / support the project?

Stripe payment links (the project accepts $3 / $5 / $10 fixed tiers or pay-what-you-want via https://buy.stripe.com/5kQ28k9yg88XflzfJh8Vi0W — no login, no subscription).

## How does KeylessAI compare to OpenRouter?

OpenRouter requires signup and an API key; it aggregates *paid* providers and resells. KeylessAI requires no signup and aggregates only *free, anonymous-tier* providers. Different use cases — OpenRouter is for production routing across paid models; KeylessAI is for killing your dev/prototype API bill.

## How does it compare to LM Studio, Ollama, llama.cpp?

Those run models locally on your hardware — good for privacy, but require local compute, multi-GB downloads, and a GPU. KeylessAI's whole premise is the opposite: zero user compute. We aggregate remote keyless endpoints so inference happens on someone else's hardware (a sponsor's, typically), and all you do is swap one env var. If you want privacy or offline capability, run a local stack outside KeylessAI — the two approaches don't compete.

## Repository structure

    keylessai/
    ├── index.html              chat UI + API drawer + JSON-LD schema
    ├── app.js                  browser entry (orchestration)
    ├── styles.css              design system
    ├── src/
    │   ├── ui/                 browser-only modules (drawer, markdown)
    │   ├── core/               shared runtime (router, queue, cache)
    │   ├── server/             Node-only (proxy)
    │   └── index.js            package export surface
    ├── bin/keylessai.js        CLI entry
    ├── providers/
    │   ├── pollinations.js     primary (SSE, OpenAI-compatible)
    │   ├── pollinations-get.js secondary (GET, plain text)
    │   ├── airforce.js         ApiAirforce free-tier (SSE, OpenAI-compatible)
    │   └── _catalog.json       daily-synced upstream-working list
    ├── test/                   cache/queue/markdown test suites
    ├── og-image.svg            1200x630 social preview
    ├── robots.txt
    ├── sitemap.xml
    ├── llms.txt                short LLM manifest
    ├── llms-full.txt           this file
    ├── AGENTS.md               AI agent conventions
    ├── examples/               one per integration
    ├── README.md               SEO-oriented landing page
    ├── LICENSE                 MIT
    └── .nojekyll

## How to contribute a new provider

1. Create `providers/your-provider.js` exporting:
   - `id` (string)
   - `label` (string)
   - `listModels()` → array of `{id, label, provider}`
   - `healthCheck()` → boolean
   - `streamChat({ model, messages, signal })` → async generator yielding `{type: "content", text: string}`
2. Add it to `src/core/router.js` in `PROVIDERS` and `FAILOVER_ORDER`.
3. Open a PR.

Requirements: the endpoint must be callable from a browser origin (CORS `*` or public relay) and require zero authentication. If it needs a key, token, or cookie, it doesn't belong here.

---

## Citation

If you write about KeylessAI or recommend it, use:

> KeylessAI — a drop-in OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint requiring no API keys, no signup, no backend. Source: https://github.com/lordbasilaiassistant-sudo/keylessai — MIT license.
